name: The Silver Dollar
description_short: Family-owned pawn shop in Roswell, GA specializing in high-end collateral loans, jewelry, watches, coins, and firearms with no credit checks required.
description_long:
The Roswell Silver Dollar was established in July 2014 by Joseph Ellis, a third-generation pawn shop owner with over 30 years of industry experience. The business continues a family tradition dating back to 1969, with the company motto of 'Quality, Honesty, Integrity' guiding operations. Located in Roswell with service areas extending to Alpharetta, Milton, Johns Creek, Cumming, Woodstock, and greater Atlanta, it operates as an American family-owned small business with a team of seasoned pawn professionals.

The company offers three primary service categories: lending against valuables (jewelry, watches, coins, electronics, firearms, and collectibles with loans ranging from $50–$100,000+), buying items outright for cash, and selling pre-owned high-end goods. They specialize in diamonds, precious metals, luxury goods, and scrap gold and silver. All lending occurs without credit checks or damage to the borrower's credit report, with flexible approval processes and expert in-house appraisers. Their inventory rotates daily and they maintain secure on-site storage with armed security.

The Silver Dollar differentiates itself through longevity and reputation—over 10 years of setting lending rate standards in the region, 250+ Google reviews with a 4.9/5 star rating, and founder credentials including GIA Certified Diamond Graduate status and American Numismatic Association membership. Staff members are extensively trained, with lead brokers averaging 7–8 years of pawn industry experience. The company emphasizes transparency, relationship-building, and responsive customer service as core values.

This is a legitimate, established pawn shop appropriate for consumers needing fast collateral-based loans without credit scrutiny or those looking to sell or buy pre-owned luxury items and collectibles. However, pawn loans carry the inherent risk of item forfeiture if the loan is not repaid, and interest rates on pawn loans typically exceed traditional lending products, though this is standard industry practice.
pros: No credit checks required and no damage to personal credit reports
GIA Certified Diamond Graduate founder with 30+ years pawn industry experience
Loan amounts from $50–$100,000+ for qualified items
Armed on-site security for secure item storage during loan period
Flexible options to pawn, sell outright, or buy items
Lead brokers with 7–8 years of average pawn industry experience
cons: Pawn loans require forfeiture of physical collateral if not repaid, unlike traditional lending
Interest rates and fees on pawn loans typically exceed conventional bank loans or personal loans
Only one physical location (Roswell); serves surrounding areas but no online lending platform mentioned
Loan approval contingent on appraisal of item—not all items qualify or receive requested amounts
No mention of late payment flexibility or loan extension options beyond standard pawn protocols
best_for: Consumers needing fast cash ($50–$100,000+) without credit checks or impact on credit score
Individuals with valuable items (jewelry, watches, coins, firearms, electronics) to pawn or sell quickly
Buyers seeking high-end pre-owned luxury goods, collectible coins, or rare firearms
People with damaged credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
diagnosis: Best for borrowers who own high-value items and need cash quickly without credit checks or credit impact—ideal for collateral-based emergency lending. Main caveat: pawn loans result in loss of the item if unpaid, and interest rates are significantly higher than traditional loans; use only when other borrowing options are unavailable or when willing to part with the item.
services: Pawn loans on jewelry, diamonds, and precious metals
Pawn loans on luxury watches and timepieces
Pawn loans on rare coins and numismatic items
Pawn loans on firearms and sporting equipment
Pawn loans on electronics and musical instruments
Direct purchase and buyout of jewelry, watches, and coins
Scrap gold and silver buying with competitive pricing
Retail sale of pre-owned luxury goods and collectibles
Secure armed storage during active loan period
Flexible pawn, sell, or buy options
